name: Amphotericin B deoxycholate
executiveSummary: Febrile neutropenia on day 7. Pulmonary infiltrate during neutropenia. Documented systemic mycoses.
indications:
## Main indications

Febrile neutropenia on day 7. Pulmonary infiltrate during neutropenia. Documented systemic mycoses.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 50 mg (Anfotericina LIA, Richet, Northia**).
adulto.reconstitution: 10 mL SWFI. Conc: 5 mg/mL. Shake vial until colloidal solution is clear.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute reconstituted solution in 500 mL 5% dextrose and administer over 6 h.
adulto.notes: Do not use 0.9% NaCl (may precipitate). Under no circumstances should the dose exceed 1,5 mg/kg/day. Overdose may cause cardiorespiratory arrest.

pediatrico.reconstitution: Reconstitute with SWFI. Dilute with dextrose (NOT saline).
pediatrico.infusionRate: Between 2 and 4 hours, with continuous infusion pump.
pediatrico.dose: Start 0,5 mg/kg/day, increase 0,2 mg/kg/day every 24 h, up to 1 mg/kg/day, maximum dose: 50 mg/day. Rapid administration method: start 0,25 mg/kg and increase at 6 h intervals up to 1 mg/kg/day. Do not exceed 1,5 mg/kg/day.
pediatrico.notes: Infuse over 4–6 h at concentration of 0,1 mg/mL in 5% dextrose via peripheral line and up to 0,5 mg/mL via central line. Premedicate to reduce adverse reactions with hydrocortisone, paracetamol, diphenhydramine 30 min before infusion. Do not administer with solutions containing electrolytes. Correct hypokalemia before administration.

neonatal.dose: 0,5–1 mg/kg/dose every 24–48 h per NICU protocol.
neonatal.administration: Central IV with hydration and electrolyte monitoring.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 24 h at room temperature in the dark and 7 days refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- Diluted solution is not stable. If remainder, discard. Use light-protected infusion set.

## Pediatric guide — Reconstituted

- Once reconstituted: 24 h at room temperature, 7 days refrigerated (2–8°C).
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Fever, chills, vomiting (infusion-related), hypokalemia (25%), hypomagnesemia. Nephrotoxicity: may be prevented with prior saline infusion. Anemia (75%). Rare: leukopenia, thrombocytopenia.
